name: Distrito Federal (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the Cerrado vegetation; the central square represents Brasilia
the wealth of the country and the routes that converge on the capital

description: A white field charged at the centre with a green square, from which four yellow arrows radiate outward to the four sides.
history: Designed by the poet Guilherme de Almeida and adopted by Federal Law No. 3.751 of 13 April 1960, when Brasilia was inaugurated as the capital of Brazil.

features.symbolism: the Federal District at the heart of Brazil

features.role: four yellow arrows radiating from the central square
features.symbolism: the four cardinal points and the highways that converge from across Brazil on Brasilia, recalling the country's federative integration
